summary: Package exfiltrates SSH directory contents to hardcoded remote IP
details: The package advertises itself as a date-formatting utility but ships postinstall.js which reads the contents of the installer's ~/.ssh directory, collects public-key filenames along with the OS username and platform, and POSTs the JSON payload over HTTPS to the hardcoded IP 124.221.154.135:443/post. In-source Chinese comments explicitly self-describe the destination as a C2 server and announce 'data collection complete'. The file is named postinstall.js (an npm lifecycle hook name) but package.json wires it to a non-standard 'prebuild' script — so it does not auto-fire on `npm install`, but it executes whenever a consumer runs the build, invokes the script manually, or if the maintainer renames the entry to 'postinstall'. The reading of ~/.ssh is wholly incompatible with the package's stated date-formatting purpose.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
